summary.intent: Build a complete browser-in-browser viewing UI for Moltis's Settings > Browser page, enabling live CDP screencast viewing with full mouse/keyboard/scroll interaction, session management, cookie persistence, and chat integration.
summary.outcome: Delivered a fully functional interactive browser viewer with live streaming, session history (SQLite), per-agent cookie profiles, URL autocomplete, paste support, stealth Chrome flags, and 55+ e2e tests. PR #531 created with ~70 commits.

repo: WebSocket v4 protocol requires explicit event subscription — events not in the subscription list are silently dropped by the broadcast filter in gateway/src/broadcast.rs
BrowserManager uses Arc<OnceCell<Arc<BrowserManager>>> for lazy initialization — manager() is async and inits on first use, so hooks must be registered inside the init closure, not at startup
The gateway has two separate BrowserManager instances (BrowserTool and RealBrowserService) that must share one via Arc<OnceCell> for sessions to be visible in the UI
biome organize-imports can create duplicate import names when merging separate import statements — must check after biome --write runs
The settings router strips query strings from paths, so session IDs must be passed via module-level variables (pendingSelectSession) rather than URL params
Chrome profile SingletonLock is a symlink, and exists() follows symlinks returning false for dangling ones — must use symlink_metadata() for cleanup
CDP mouseWheel requires BOTH deltaX and deltaY — omitting either causes 'deltaX and deltaY are expected' error and silent scroll failure
Canvas event listeners must use ref callbacks (not useEffect with [] deps) when canvas is conditionally rendered — useEffect fires once before canvas exists
The worktree path is /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/claude/plan-browser-viewing-HOHno, data dir is .moltis/

code.finding: Original relay exited on tx.send() with no receivers — caused 'Waiting for first frame' race condition. Fixed by ignoring SendError instead of breaking
action_name from Display impl includes parameters ('mouse_input(x=1,y=2)'), so exact string matching for cleanup decisions fails silently — must use starts_with()
RealBrowserService uses session_store: OnceCell and action_hook: OnceCell so they can be set after db_pool is created (which happens after service construction)
BrowserInstance.browser changed to Arc<Browser> for tab reuse — same profile_id creates new tab in existing browser instance, sharing cookies without profile lock conflicts
browser.screencast.frame must be in the eventHandlers subscription list passed to the server — it was missing, causing all screencast frames to be silently dropped
inline-block on scrollbar wrapper div prevents proper height containment for aspect-ratio canvas — scrollbar renders below canvas. Must use block display (default div)
BrowserConfig.sandbox field only affects RealBrowserService (UI path). BrowserTool uses SandboxRouter and needs sandbox_override: Option<bool> set from config to respect sandbox=false
CHROME_FLAGS env var passes flags to browserless/chrome container's Chrome process — used to add --restore-session-cookies and stealth flags

workflow: local-validate.sh uses system biome (v2.4.6), NOT npx biome (v0.3.3) — formatting must use system biome or results won't match CI
YubiKey GPG signing blocks background tasks — foreground commits required or user must tap before task runs
Chrome SharedProfile strategy: same profile_id = new tab in existing browser (Arc<Browser>), different profile_id = new container. One container per profile, multiple tabs per container
CDP EventFrameNavigated eliminates polling for URL changes; screencast frame metadata (scroll_offset_y) eliminates polling for scroll position
Rapid stop/start of CDP screencasts overwhelms Chrome — never call stop_screencast on session switch, use per-session frame filtering in the JS listener instead

friction: YubiKey GPG signing blocked multiple background git commits — required repeated manual taps and foreground retries
biome organize-imports silently created duplicate 'navigate' import names when merging new imports into existing lines, causing runtime SyntaxErrors
Chrome SingletonLock as dangling symlink caused Chrome to refuse to start — very hard to debug because exists() returned false for a file that was blocking Chrome
LinkedIn's Chrome container kept timing out and freezing (CDPErrors, ERR_NETWORK_CHANGED) due to container networking overhead and memory pressure from JS-heavy pages
The action hook registration timing issue (manager lazy-inits after startup) meant session history was silently not working — no errors, just empty history
Canvas aspect-ratio + inline-block creates scrollbar positioning failure that looks correct in code but breaks in rendering
axum path syntax change (:param → {param}) caused a runtime panic, not a compile error
Agent (MiniMax M2.7) kept misinterpreting what was visible in screenshots (describing sign-in page when user was logged in) — not a code issue but significant workflow friction
open_items: WebRTC upgrade for sub-100ms latency (vs current 100-300ms CDP screencast) — neko/webrtc-rs identified as right approach
Binary WebSocket frames instead of base64 JSON for ~33% bandwidth reduction
Video recording/replay of browser sessions (JPEG snapshots on key actions were mentioned but not implemented)
Container memory/disk monitoring in Settings UI (user asked, explicitly deferred)
Chat screenshot persistence across page reloads (screenshots are transient media/ files, not persisted)
Per-session browser profiles UI allowing named profiles from config rather than auto-derived from session key
The 55+ e2e tests added cover UI interactions but require a live server — no mock/stub infrastructure exists for truly isolated browser feature testing
The scrollbar click-to-scroll feature uses estimated page height (from evaluate on navigate) rather than live updates — page height can change as content loads
Profile sharing means concurrent sessions in same profile can have cookie race conditions during heavy writes
